_meta.deliverable: Comprehensive implementation-level todo plan for a map-first track randomization revamp
_meta.planning_mode: TDD-first, tooling-first phase 1, clean break allowed
_meta.primary_goal: Replace the current curve-first track generator with a map-first pipeline that starts from generated map geometry, derives curve data from that geometry, avoids self-intersections by default, and supports a rare single grade-separated crossing case.
source_inputs: User requirements gathered via ask tool before planning

current_pipeline: The current track flow is curve-first: generate curve RLE, derive slopes, place signs/features from curve windows, generate minimap last, then score preview constraints.
Current orchestration in tools/randomizer/track_pipeline.js keeps each track on its original track_length budget even though older docs still describe variable length generation.
Current minimap and preview tooling assume curve data is the geometry source of truth and evaluate curve-vs-minimap agreement as a quality signal.
Current validators are strong on structural/runtime safety, but topology is mostly reported as preview metrics rather than enforced as hard pass/fail rules.
known_conflicts_with_map_first: docs/randomizer_architecture.md and existing tests explicitly describe minimap generation as derived from curve data; that assumption must be retired.
Sign planning and preview scoring are curve-window driven today; both need geometry-derived feature anchors instead.
Several baselines and snapshots encode current curve-first preview behavior and will drift intentionally during the revamp.
track_validator.js has no hard geometry-topology gate for zero crossings vs one grade-separated crossing.
known_crossing_constraints: The original Japan track is the best in-repo reference for a crossing, but the exact bridge/underpass representation is not fully documented yet.
The runtime minimap position stream is only signed (x, y) pairs; no explicit z-layer field was found.
Tunnel-related special behavior exists in runtime/sign handling, while a generic bridge/overpass data model was not clearly identified.
Phase 1 should stay tooling/data-first, with runtime/ASM changes only escalated if tooling prototypes cannot make the crossing readable or behaviorally correct.

locked_decisions.point_density: Use a configurable point density derived from map dimensions rather than a single fixed global point count.
locked_decisions.hamilton_cycle_scope: The generated cycle must visit every sampled point exactly once.
locked_decisions.smoothing_policy: Use a clamp-until-valid smoothing loop; if a smoothing pass risks a self-intersection, reduce smoothing until the path remains valid.
locked_decisions.crossing_probability: Roll a deterministic 1-in-16 chance per randomized track for the optional crossing case.
locked_decisions.crossing_style: The upper branch should feel visually normal; the lower branch must read as driving through a tunnel or under a bridge.
locked_decisions.todo_depth: Implementation-level tasks with research gates, TDD milestones, and explicit validation steps.
locked_decisions.compatibility: Treat the revamp as a clean break in generation behavior; preserving existing seeds/output shape is not a goal.
locked_decisions.runtime_scope: Phase 1 stays tooling-first; only escalate to runtime/ASM work after explicit research and prototype gates.
locked_decisions.crossing_metadata: Do not plan on new persisted crossing/layer metadata in tools/data/tracks.json unless a later escalation proves it unavoidable.
locked_decisions.track_coverage: Target all 19 track slots, including Monaco arcade variants and Monaco Arcade Wet sync behavior.
locked_decisions.japan_research: Make reverse-engineering of the original Japan crossing a required early milestone before implementation claims are made.

principles: Add direct geometry/topology tests before extracting or rewriting generation helpers.
Freeze or replace compact baselines before intentionally changing algorithmic behavior.
Prefer focused synthetic geometry fixtures for intersection edge cases instead of relying only on end-to-end randomized seeds.
Promote topology from a preview metric to a hard validation rule once the geometry pipeline is in place.

new_suites_to_add.purpose: Unit tests for segment intersection, loop closure, resampling, and geometric helper functions.
Hard tests for zero-crossing loops, a single allowed crossing, shared endpoints, near misses, and multiple-crossing rejection.
Integration tests for point sampling, Hamilton cycle generation, smoothing clamp behavior, and deterministic geometry output.
Tests for geometry-to-curve, geometry-to-slope, and geometry-to-minimap projection rules.

global_acceptance: The new source of truth for randomized track shape is generated map geometry, not curve data.
Non-crossing tracks have zero non-endpoint self-intersections after sampling, smoothing, and minimap projection.
Crossing tracks occur with deterministic 1/16 probability and contain exactly one grade-separated crossing, never more.
Any crossing track has distinct upper/lower heights at the crossing and clearly marks the lower branch as tunnel or under-bridge.
The generated minimap keeps a visible border around the road and makes the above/below relationship legible at the crossing rather than merging branches into one blob.
All 19 track slots still randomize successfully, including Monaco Arcade Wet inheriting shared geometry from Monaco Arcade Main.
Same seed and flags remain deterministic across independent runs.
All new and updated tests pass, workspace-safe randomization still works, and canonical root verify remains clean after non-randomized source changes.
guardrails: Do not add Python or Jupyter tooling; all new helper modules and tests stay in Node.js/JavaScript.
Keep master/root buildable and close each checkpoint with canonical PowerShell verify.bat when repo files outside workspace outputs change.
Prefer workspace-safe flows and use in-root randomizer runs only for deliberate debugging.
Do not persist new crossing metadata in tools/data/tracks.json during phase 1 unless a later escalation gate approves it.
Keep the injectable track schema stable unless a schema change is explicitly justified and covered by new tests/docs.
Treat docs that still say minimap count is (track_length >> 5) + 1 as stale and update them as part of the revamp.

phases.title: Research And Contracts
phases.goal: Freeze the current behavior, document the curve-first assumptions that must be replaced, and lock the map-first design contracts before major refactoring starts.
exit_criteria: Japan crossing behavior has a concrete research note with knowns, unknowns, and phase-1-safe assumptions.
The internal geometry canvas, distance budget contract, and source-of-truth object are specified.
Stale minimap-size documentation is identified and queued for correction.

tasks.title: Freeze compact baselines and catalog curve-first assumptions
acceptance: Current curve-first outputs are frozen or intentionally replaced with a note that map-first drift is expected.
A short inventory exists of every current assumption that treats curves as the geometry authority.

tasks.title: Reverse-engineer the original Japan crossing before implementation claims
acceptance: The repo has a concrete note describing how much of Japan's crossing is data-driven versus runtime-driven.
Tunnel/underpass-related sign and tileset behavior near the crossing is identified well enough to guide phase 1 tooling work.
Unknowns that would require later ASM work are called out explicitly rather than guessed.

tasks.title: Define the map-first geometry contract and internal canvas
acceptance: A single in-memory geometry object is defined as the source of truth for randomized track shape.
The geometry canvas is explicitly tied to map/minimap dimensions and margins instead of being implicit.
Track length policy for the map-first pipeline is written down before implementation branches out.

tasks.title: Resolve minimap pair-count contract and stale docs
acceptance: The source-of-truth rule for minimap pair count is track_length >> 6 everywhere in docs, tests, and validation.
No remaining revamp task depends on the stale (track_length >> 5) + 1 formula.

phases.title: Geometry TDD Foundation
phases.goal: Build a reliable geometry layer and topology test bed before replacing generator internals.
exit_criteria: There are direct tests for all target intersection edge cases.
Reusable geometry helpers exist for intersection tests, closure, smoothing checks, and resampling.
Topology can be classified without adding persisted crossing metadata to tools/data/tracks.json.

tasks.title: Add synthetic geometry fixtures and topology-first tests
acceptance: Tests cover no-crossing loops, a single true crossing, multiple crossings, shared endpoints, near-touch cases, and deterministic replay.
The fixtures are simple and explicit enough to debug topology failures without involving full randomizer runs.

tasks.title: Implement reusable geometry primitives
acceptance: The new geometry helper module provides segment intersection, closure checks, path length, sampling, and resampling helpers.
The helpers are deterministic and safe to reuse from the generator, validator, and minimap tooling.

tasks.title: Implement topology report model and hard crossing rules
acceptance: Topology reports expose crossing count, crossing locations, and whether the track is currently eligible for the single-crossing rule.
Default validation fails any non-endpoint self-intersection unless the later grade-separated crossing rule is explicitly satisfied by derived geometry/elevation data.
The persisted track JSON schema remains unchanged during this phase.

tasks.title: Prepare track model and test helpers for transient geometry work state
acceptance: Generation code can carry in-memory geometry candidates and preview data without polluting the injectable track schema.
Schema tests make it explicit which fields are transient and which fields must survive injection.

phases.title: Map Skeleton And Smoothing
phases.goal: Generate a deterministic, non-self-intersecting closed map skeleton from sampled points, then smooth and resample it without breaking validity.
exit_criteria: Point sampling is dimension-aware and deterministic.
The Hamilton cycle visits every sampled point exactly once and closes cleanly.
Smoothing can improve shape while clamping back to the last valid path whenever needed.

tasks.title: Implement configurable point sampling inside the map canvas
acceptance: Point density is configurable from map dimensions and constrained by minimum spacing and edge margins.
Repeated runs with the same seed produce the same point set for the same track slot.

tasks.title: Build a Hamilton cycle generator that visits every sampled point
acceptance: The cycle visits all sampled points exactly once before returning to the start.
The base cycle is non-self-intersecting in the default case and exposes retry diagnostics instead of silently dropping points.
Failure/retry budgets are deterministic and test-covered.

tasks.title: Add clamp-until-valid smoothing and closure preservation
acceptance: Smoothing never introduces an unapproved self-intersection; if it would, the algorithm clamps to a weaker valid smoothing level.
Loop closure, map bounds, and start/finish orientation survive smoothing.

tasks.title: Resample the smoothed loop to a stable track-distance budget
acceptance: A deterministic resampled centerline exists for downstream curve, slope, sign, and minimap projection.
The start/finish straight remains readable and safe after resampling.

tasks.title: Replace preview candidate scoring with geometry-quality scoring
acceptance: Candidate selection no longer depends on curve-to-minimap agreement as the primary score.
Geometry quality metrics include topology validity, start-line safety, curvature sanity, and map fit.

phases.title: Project Geometry Into Game Data
phases.goal: Turn the map-first centerline into valid SMGP curve, slope, sign, and minimap data while keeping the new geometry as the authoritative shape.
exit_criteria: Curve projection produces valid curve bytes and valid RLE segments.
Slope generation reflects geometry/elevation decisions and supports future crossing separation.
Minimap generation and preview assets are derived directly from geometry rather than reconstructed from curves.

tasks.title: Project map geometry into curve bytes and curve RLE
acceptance: Projected curve streams match the required decompressed length and valid byte ranges.
RLE output stays within RAM/runtime limits and remains deterministic for fixed geometry.

tasks.title: Generate elevation, visual slopes, and physical slopes from geometry
acceptance: Zero-crossing tracks get safe visual/physical slope streams that pass current runtime envelopes.
The projection model is ready to enforce vertical separation at the crossing once the crossing case is enabled.

tasks.title: Rebuild minimap position and preview generation from geometry
acceptance: Minimap pairs come directly from geometry sampling instead of reintegrated curve bytes.
Generated minimap output fits panel constraints and retains a readable road border.

tasks.title: Refactor sign and special-road planning to use geometry features
acceptance: Sign placement is anchored to geometry-derived straights, turns, and special-road windows rather than raw curve windows.
Wraparound spacing and tileset transitions remain runtime-safe.

phases.title: Optional Single Crossing
phases.goal: Enable the rare grade-separated crossing case without compromising the default non-self-intersecting generator.
exit_criteria: The crossing case is deterministic, rare, and capped at exactly one crossing.
The lower branch is clearly below the upper branch in generated elevation and feature planning.
The minimap/rendered map communicates above/below order without persisted crossing metadata in track JSON.

tasks.title: Add deterministic 1-in-16 crossing selection
acceptance: Crossing eligibility is deterministic per seed and track slot.
Large-sample tests show an empirical crossing rate close to 1/16 without affecting non-crossing determinism.

tasks.title: Inject exactly one grade-separated crossing into the geometry pipeline
acceptance: When the crossing roll hits, the geometry contains exactly one non-endpoint crossing and never more than one.
All sampled points are still visited exactly once in the closed cycle.
Smoothing and resampling preserve the single-crossing cap.

tasks.title: Make above/below ordering inferable from geometry and elevation
acceptance: Crossing tracks derive a clear upper branch and lower branch from geometry/elevation, without adding new persisted fields to tools/data/tracks.json.
The lower branch receives tunnel or under-bridge handling, while the upper branch remains visually normal.

tasks.title: Make crossing depiction legible in minimap/map assets while preserving the border
acceptance: At the crossing, both branches keep a visible outer border instead of merging into a single filled shape.
The generated map makes it visually obvious which branch is above and which branch is below.

tasks.title: Open a runtime/ASM escalation only if tooling-only prototypes fail
acceptance: Phase 1 remains tooling-first unless a written escalation note shows exactly why asset/data work cannot achieve readable or behaviorally correct crossings.
Any candidate ASM work is narrowed to specific runtime files and specific missing behaviors.

phases.title: Validation, Rollout, And Documentation
phases.goal: Make the new pipeline the supported default, replace stale curve-first assumptions in tests/docs, and verify the full workspace-safe flow.
exit_criteria: Topology is enforced as a validator rule, not just a preview metric.
All relevant tests, baselines, and docs reflect the map-first pipeline.
All 19 tracks build and validate in workspace-safe mode.

tasks.title: Promote topology and crossing rules to hard validation and CLI reporting
acceptance: A track fails validation if it has an unapproved self-intersection.
A crossing track passes only when it has exactly one crossing and the derived upper/lower separation checks succeed.
CLI JSON reports surface crossing count and classification explicitly.

tasks.title: Update existing suites and baselines to remove curve-first assumptions
acceptance: Tests no longer assume minimap is secondary to curves.
Intentional baseline drift is documented rather than silently accepted.
Smoke and slow suites assert topology validity for both non-crossing and crossing outcomes.

tasks.title: Roll out the pipeline across all 19 tracks and preserve Monaco wet sync
acceptance: All 19 track slots randomize successfully under the new pipeline.
Monaco Arcade Wet continues to share the intended geometry/data with Monaco Arcade Main.
Selected-track mode and workspace-safe CLI behavior stay deterministic.

tasks.title: Refresh architecture, guide, and workflow docs for the map-first design
acceptance: Docs describe the map-first source of truth, the new geometry stages, the 1/16 crossing rule, and the topology validation rules.
The guide explains current limitations and the runtime-escalation rule instead of implying bridges are already a documented generic system.

tasks.title: Run full validation and prepare manual emulator spot-checks
acceptance: The full automated test matrix passes after the map-first rollout.
Workspace-safe randomized build succeeds without mutating the repo root.
A manual emulator checklist exists for at least one non-crossing seed and one crossing seed once stable examples are available.

risk_register.risk: Hamilton cycle generation over arbitrary sampled points may be brittle or expensive.
mitigation: Keep deterministic retry budgets and diagnostics in TRR-021.
Constrain point sampling and spacing first in TRR-020 instead of solving arbitrary worst cases.

risk_register.risk: Spline smoothing may reintroduce intersections even when the base polygon is valid.
mitigation: Keep direct topology tests for pre-smooth and post-smooth paths in TRR-010 and TRR-022.

risk_register.risk: A crossing may be geometrically valid but not readable enough in minimap/map assets without runtime help.
mitigation: Require Japan research first in TRR-002.
Treat runtime/ASM work as an explicit escalation gate in TRR-044 instead of guessing early.

risk_register.risk: Removing curve-first assumptions will invalidate many existing baselines and reports at once.
mitigation: Freeze and annotate baseline drift in TRR-001.
Stage validator/reporting updates in TRR-050 and TRR-051 rather than changing everything implicitly.

risk_register.risk: Trying to infer crossing semantics without persisted metadata may be harder after projection back into game formats.
mitigation: Keep transient in-memory geometry/elevation artifacts during generation in TRR-013.
Only consider persisted metadata if TRR-042 and TRR-050 prove the no-new-metadata constraint is unworkable.

trigger_conditions: Tooling-only generated assets cannot make above/below order legible at the crossing while keeping the road border intact.
Existing tunnel/sign/tileset behavior cannot reliably represent the lower branch as an underpass.
Validator/reporting cannot infer the single-crossing semantics from geometry plus projected data strongly enough to be trustworthy.
